quotes.verbatim_quote:
Fourth intervals have become extremely popular among improvisers, in recent years, both because
the interval interests them and because fourths tend to break away from the sound and rigid fun-
ction of thirds heard in ordinary ruminations on chords built in thirds.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This defines the musical rationale for studying fourth intervals — breaking from chord-thirds — which is the conceptual foundation for the entire section's exercises.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Successive perfect fourth in-
tervals will be discussed later, as they are applied to “free form” improvisation.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Flags that pure successive-fourths usage is a distinct advanced topic, distinguishing it from the mixed-interval fourth patterns taught here.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The following
studies will help prepare the student to play general fourth intervals (sometimes perfect, sometimes
augmented ) as they are determined by the tones of major and minor scales.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Specifies the rule governing interval quality in these exercises — the scale (not a fixed interval size) determines whether each fourth is perfect or augmented.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Play this in all keys. Do the same for the succeeding patterns in this section.
quotes.why_load_bearing: A blanket transposition directive that applies to every exercise in the section, making full-key fluency a non-negotiable part of the method.
